CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Dec-28-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Steven J. Patti of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and moving party Thomas Yore.
Gilliam F. Stewart of HUSCH BLACKWELL LLC (510-768-0654), appearing for defendant and opposing party Honeywell International Inc and Morse TEC, LLC as Joinder to opposing party Honeywell International Inc.
Mariam Pambukyan of WFBM, LLP (714-634-2522), appearing for defendants General Electric Company and Paramount Global.
Robert J. Bugatto of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant Foster Wheeler LLC.
Jeremy D. Milbrodt of PRINDLE, GOETZ, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (562-436-3946), appearing for Intervenor, Hartford Accident and Idemnity Company.
Fred Gatt of LEADER, BERKON, COLAO & SILVERSTEIN LLP (213-234-1750), appearing for defendant Imo Industries, Inc.
John Ybarra of POND NORTH LLP (213-617-6169), appearing for defendant FMC Corporation.
Randall K. Bernard of HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG (415-766-3200) appearing for defendant Harley-Davidson Motor Company, Inc.
Richard S. Chon of GORDON & REES LLP (510-463-8600), appearing for defendant Air & Liquid Systems Corporation fka Buffalo Pumps, LLC.

RULING:
Not contested, not argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). Opposition filed by Defendant Honeywell International, Inc. Joinder to opposition filed by Morse TEC LLC, f/k/a Borgwarner Morse TEC LLC, as Successor-by-Merger to Borg-Warner Corporation. Reply filed by Plaintiff.

The trial date is April 15, 2024 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is April 26, 2024. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court.

The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 11, 2024. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Fact discovery cut-off date is March 29, 2024. Expert discovery cut-off date is April 12, 2024.
All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than January 25, 2024. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ISSUANCE OF TENTATIVE RULING ONLY)
Off Calendar Dec-28-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than January 11, 2024, Plaintiff must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of Plaintiff's claims as to each defendant. No later than January 11, 2024, Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of Defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown.

A further case management conference is set for February 22, 2024 at 1:30 p.m. There is no need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar.
Plaintiff is to make a settlement demand on Defendants no later than March 1, 2024. Responses to the demand are due no later than March 15, 2024. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Pang Ly via electronic mail by April 5, 2024 at 12:00 p.m. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, April 11, 2024 at 9:00 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Samuel K. Feng, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/SKF)
